title: 5 factors of action | Bhagavad Gita 18.15 - 18.18 | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai.
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Today we'll be covering four verses from Bhagavad Gita, chapter number 18, text number 15 to 18.
The topic is five factors of action. Before we start, anyone can help me with five factors,
names? Anyone has read? Endeavor? The doer? So if we discuss this now, then there will
be vacuum. When we hear this, it will go in. What are the five factors? For any work to
accomplish, what are the five?
The five important ingredients, which contribute to the accomplishment of that work.
Enthusiasm, hard work, patience, determination, inspiration, plan, strategy.
The way. The way. Okay. Well. Okay. So we will. So based on teachings of Srila Prabhupada,
we'll be discussing this particular topic, five factors of action. So text number 15.
Whatever right or wrong action a man performs by body, mind or speech is caused by the body.
These five factors. Whatever action we perform, right or wrong, are caused by these five
factors. So before we get into the discussion of what are these five factors, we all have
understanding of right and wrong. And we all will agree it's a very subjective thing,
right and wrong. You agree with what I'm saying?
What is right for one person is wrong for another person. What is wrong for one person
is right for another person. Am I right? Give me an example. What is right to eat in Wuhan
is wrong to eat in India. Anyone has seen that WhatsApp video of the marketplace in
Wuhan, which was snakes, all the things. How many of you have seen that? How many of you
have seen that? It's right there. It's right to eat. And there is one very young child
taking the soup of bat. For many people, beef. Perfectly right. What's wrong? And for us,
oh no. Oh no. Even if a person were eating chicken, no, I don't eat.
Beef. For some people, pork is right. For Muslim, it's wrong. So who defines what is
right? What is wrong? Mind. Okay, tell me one thing. We say wrong action means there
will be reaction. Papa. And as a result, you will have to suffer.
And good action means, Punya means the reaction will be prosperity, happiness, good reaction.
Now law of karma varies from country to country. Varies from or it depends on what our understanding
is of right and wrong. Yes, no. How many of you have said yes? Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes,
no. Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes,
no. Yes, no. Yes, no. Yes, no. The law of karma, what reaction you get. Is it dependent
on what you think as right or wrong? Or irrespective of what you think is right or wrong, it is
a well defined law. How many of you have said it's a well defined law? For example, I go
to the US. I'm quite comfortable driving on the left side. I think, I think in my opinion,
it is much better, it's much more comfortable, it's much more safer to drive on the left
side. I think so. In my opinion, that is better. Am I allowed? If I drive on the left side,
will I be penalized? So very fundamental question to you all, before we get into actions. Right
or wrong, is it left to my judgment, my opinion, my thinking or irrespective what I think right
or wrong, there is some actions which are right, some actions which are wrong. How many
of us say irrespective what I think right or wrong, there are some actions are wrong,
some actions are right, basis which will get reaction.
Now, who defines what is right, what is wrong? Scriptures also vary. Quran says purk is bad.
Our Vedic literature says everything is bad. And some gurus of different religion talks
about different things. Are you all with me, what we are discussing? Rajivji, is it clear,
what we are discussing?

They also trust.
Quran and this purk is right.

think it is wrong, if you feel guilt-filling, don't eat.
know it, there will be a bad reaction. And just like Prabhupada gives an example, a child

Nothing.
Chalaan kada. So, in human form of life, we are leading our lives with our own understanding of

ek aadmi ko maine tang nahi kiya, sab ka bhala hi socha hai maine, lekin mujhe kyun saffar karne ko mil raha hai.

So right and wrong is not subject to our understanding or our opinion. There are higher laws, the
laws of universal government just like in Mumbai you have the laws of Maharashtra government

there are some regulations.
And some religions are very evolved just like mathematics in some classes it's very evolved

So all religions are basically wanting us to take to that truth. Understanding what
is the right, what is wrong.

Is it clear?
It's not wrong. It's not wrong if they are following the system because just like is

Is it wrong to tell a child 2 minus 5 is not possible.
A teacher in elementary class will tell 2 minus 5. If you have 2 apples, how will you

If at the red light he continues moving, there is a speed breaker, he doesn't slow down,

He will mess up his life, mess up others life.
Imagine at red light, it's red light and then person just, traffic from the cross road is passing by,

He will get hurt, he will hurt others.
This world is all about that.
All misery what you see, even so called latest coronavirus, it's all accidents because people don't know the law.
That's why it says,
come to light.

So the five factors are, we talked about it last class, place of action, performer, third one, the senses, the endeavour and ultimately the super soul.
These are the five factors.
You see the place of action, many times you have heard about people saying,
this place is not good.
Have you heard of this?
And now these are Vastu compliant.
Place also matters.
Although, we as devotees many times do not give undue value to those laws knowing that the law maker is ultimately the supreme soul.
So when we go to a place, yes you can do all the rituals, go all the things, we generally do Kirtan.
Because you see these four are material causes.
The place of action, the performer, the senses, endeavour and this is the ultimate cause, Daivam, the supreme lord.
Even all these four causes are against you.
If this cause is in favour of you, you will be victorious.
Give me an example.
Krishna and Arjun.
Arjun, the Pandavas were weak.

They had people like, you know, Karna, Bhishma.

The army is much bigger.
But Krishna, the ultimate cause was with Arjun.

Text 17. One who is not motivated by false ego, whose intelligence is not entangled,
though he kills men in this world, is not the slayer, nor is he bound by his action.
Now the example is given that in a war,
a person is ordered by his colonel, by his general,
go and kill. Like India-Pakistan war happened. Then I'm sure at the battlefront in the war field,
there'll be some people who have gone and killed the enemy soldiers.
And when they come back, they get an award. Because they're following the order of superior.
When the same soldier goes back to his village and he has enmity with his neighbor, some property
dispute and takes the same very gun, which he has used to shoot the enemy soldiers, same very gun,
and one person he shoots and kills, what happens to him? He is caught. Because he is not acting
with his superior authority. Action is the same. In both cases he has pulled the trigger
and killed someone. One action is glorified, other action is implicating him, is imprisoning
him. Same is the case when the action is propelled, propelled with the intention of, with the
direction of the Supreme Lord, when it is done for the pleasure of the Supreme Lord.
Just like the general, the same action. So here it says, although he kills men in this
world, he is not the slayer. Now this does not mean that whimsical killing is allowed,
Bhagawan has said.
These jihadis, they say, they are doing this for God. Not that, not whimsical. Nor is he
bound by this action. There is no reaction to such action. That's called akarma. Arjuna's
desire not to fight was arising from false ego. Mere log, meri family, meri dynasty,
kya log sochenge, paap aayega mujhe. It was all at the false ego platform. And therefore
Arjuna says,
Krishna says, I want you to fight. Just be an instrument, take the credit. Arjuna thought
himself to be the doer of action. He was at the center of his decision making. Everything
was revolving around I and mine, my family, my dynasty, my kingdom, my prosperity, my
sinful reaction. I will suffer, revolving around I.
Actually, it should be revolving around the Lord. When everything is revolving around
I, that is false ego. Aham mameti, I and mine. When the center becomes the Lord, that is
right consciousness. Supreme Lord, as a supreme sanctioner, is perfect in doing everything.
So when a person develops that relationship, he has that faith, he has that love, he has
that consciousness, when he is awakened, then he takes everything as in stride. Even
the so-called reversals in life. There's a learning forming. The Lord wants to teach
some lesson. It's my doing. A devotee doesn't mean that there is no reversal in devotee's
life. The other day I gave an example, it is not that the devotee will not encounter
death.
This natural phenomena will happen. But because of this higher consciousness, he is unaffected.
His consciousness is purified. Personal activity and responsibility arise from false ego and
godlessness or lack of Krishna. Personal responsibility. And from false ego and godlessness. When
a person is on platform, I am the doer, then all blame comes to him. Anyone who is acting
in Krishna consciousness under the direction of the Supreme Lord, the Supersoul, he actually
is peaceful. Respective of the outcome of that activity. Success, great, credit goes
to the Lord. Failure, great, makes him more Krishna conscious, more dependent on the Lord.
